overview: A two-tier leave management system consisting of a React-based frontend (leave-web-app) for employees and managers to submit, view, and manage leave requests, and a Go backend service (leave-api) that handles business logic, leave balance calculations, approval workflows, and data persistence. Communication occurs via RESTful HTTP between the web app and the API service.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests specifying start date, end date, leave type, and reason
Managers must be able to view all pending leave requests from their direct reports
Managers must be able to approve or reject leave requests with optional comments
Employees must be able to view their remaining leave balance segmented by leave type (e.g., annual, sick, personal)
All users must be able to view the history and current status of their own leave requests
The system must validate that leave requests do not exceed available balance before submission
The system must prevent overlapping leave requests for the same employee
The API must expose a health check endpoint for Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes
The frontend must provide a responsive user interface accessible from desktop and mobile browsers
Manager-employee relationships must be maintained to enforce approval workflows
Leave request statuses must include at least: pending, approved, rejected
The system must record timestamps for all state transitions (submitted, approved, rejected)

components.name: leave-web-app
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Web Application
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend SPA for leave management system
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Application is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a TypeScript React single-page application (SPA) for leave management. Use Create React App or Vite as the build tool. The application serves on port 3000 inside the container. Implement the following routes and features: (1) /login - simple login form that captures employeeId and role (employee or manager), stores in localStorage or context. (2) /dashboard - shows different views based on role: for employees display leave balance by type, button to request new leave, and list of their own requests with status; for managers display pending requests from direct reports with approve/reject buttons. (3) /request - form to submit new leave request with fields: leaveType (dropdown: annual, sick, personal), startDate, endDate, reason (textarea). Validate dates and call POST /api/leave-requests on leave-api. (4) /history - table showing all leave requests for the logged-in user with columns: dates, type, reason, status, manager comments. All API calls go to the leave-api service via relative path /api/* (configure proxy or environment variable for base URL). Display loading states and error messages for failed API calls. Use React Router for navigation. Style with a component library like Material-UI or Ant Design. Include a /health endpoint that returns {status: 'ok'}. Build a production Docker image using multi-stage build: node:18 for build, nginx:alpine for serving static files. Configure nginx to serve index.html for all routes (SPA routing). Handle 401/403 responses by redirecting to login. Show manager comments when a request is approved or rejected. Prevent submission if employee has insufficient balance (call GET /api/employees/{id}/balance first). Display user-friendly error messages for validation failures.

components.name: leave-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Backend service for managing employee leave requests and approvals
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /api/leave-requests:
    post:
      summary: Submit a new leave request
      operationId: createLeaveRequest
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - employeeId
                - leaveType
                - startDate
                - endDate
                - reason
              properties:
                employeeId:
                  type: string
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                  enum: [annual, sick, personal]
                startDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                endDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Leave request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid request or insufficient balance
        '409':
          description: Overlapping leave request exists
    get:
      summary: Get leave requests (filtered by employeeId or managerId)
      operationId: listLeaveRequests
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
  /api/leave-requests/{id}/approve:
    post:
      summary: Approve a leave request
      operationId: approveLeaveRequest
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - managerId
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
                comments:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request approved
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Manager not authorized to approve this request
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
  /api/leave-requests/{id}/reject:
    post:
      summary: Reject a leave request
      operationId: rejectLeaveRequest
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - managerId
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
                comments:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request rejected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Manager not authorized to reject this request
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
  /api/employees/{id}/balance:
    get:
      summary: Get leave balance for an employee
      operationId: getLeaveBalance
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance by type
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  employeeId:
                    type: string
                  balances:
                    type: object
                    properties:
                      annual:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                      sick:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                      personal:
                        type: number
                        format: float
        '404':
          description: Employee not found
components:
  schemas:
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        managerId:
          type: string
        leaveType:
          type: string
          enum: [annual, sick, personal]
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        reason:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
        managerComments:
          type: string
        submittedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        reviewedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go HTTP service using net/http on port 9090. Use the standard library for routing (http.ServeMux) or a lightweight router like gorilla/mux or chi. Store data in-memory using Go maps and structs (no database required for this prototype). Define these structs: Employee (id, name, managerId, leaveBalances map[string]float64), LeaveRequest (id, employeeId, managerId, leaveType, startDate, endDate, reason, status, managerComments, submittedAt, reviewedAt). Initialize sample data: at least 5 employees with 2 managers, each employee has initial balances (annual: 20, sick: 10, personal: 5). Manager-employee relationships must be set via managerId field. Implement these endpoints: (1) GET /health returns {status: 'ok'}. (2) POST /api/leave-requests accepts JSON, validates fields, checks for overlapping requests (same employee, overlapping date range), verifies sufficient balance by calculating business days between dates and comparing to available balance, generates UUID for id, sets status to pending, captures submittedAt timestamp, deducts balance immediately (optimistic), returns 201 with created object or 400/409 on validation failure. (3) GET /api/leave-requests supports query params employeeId, managerId, status; if managerId is provided, return only requests from that manager's direct reports; if employeeId is provided, return that employee's requests; if status is provided, filter by status; return array of LeaveRequest objects. (4) POST /api/leave-requests/{id}/approve accepts managerId and optional comments, verifies the manager is the direct manager of the employee who submitted the request (403 if not), sets status to approved, captures reviewedAt timestamp, stores managerComments, returns updated LeaveRequest. (5) POST /api/leave-requests/{id}/reject accepts managerId and optional comments, verifies manager authorization same as approve, sets status to rejected, restores the deducted leave balance, captures reviewedAt, stores managerComments, returns updated LeaveRequest. (6) GET /api/employees/{id}/balance returns current balances by type for the employee. Use proper HTTP status codes. Enable CORS for frontend access. Use sync.RWMutex for thread-safe access to in-memory data. Parse dates in YYYY-MM-DD format. Calculate business days excluding weekends for balance deduction (simple heuristic: (endDate - startDate + 1) * 5/7). Log requests to stdout. Handle edge cases: invalid date ranges (end before start), non-existent employee or manager IDs (404), attempting to approve already approved/rejected requests (400). Return JSON errors with {error: 'message'} structure. Dockerfile: multi-stage build with golang:1.21 for build and alpine:latest for runtime, expose port 9090, set CMD to run the binary.
